name: Arch MCP Server
description: MCP stdio server (arch-server.mjs) that exposes the architecture wiki to Claude sessions. Cards are stored as JSON files under `<repo>/.code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json` and are tracked by git. Provides arch_list, arch_get, arch_search (BM25-ranked), and arch_upsert tools. Repo path is injected via CODE_WORKBENCH_REPO_PATH or resolved by walking up from cwd. Source lives in the @code-workbench/mcp-core package.
guidelines: Cards are plain JSON — keep them human-readable and git-diffable; avoid binary or compressed formats.
Use arch_search (BM25) for keyword lookups rather than arch_list + manual filtering.
Always set CODE_WORKBENCH_REPO_PATH when spawning the server — falling back to cwd walk is a best-effort safety net, not the primary mechanism.
Card slugs are auto-derived from the name via slugify(); use explicit slug only when the name would produce a collision or ugly slug.
Edit the server in packages/mcp-core; both the extension (esbuild bundle) and app (electron-builder) pick it up on next build.
anti_patterns: Accept a caller-supplied slug and use it verbatim in path.join — always validate slug against /^[a-z0-9-]+$/ and assert the resolved path stays inside archDir (path traversal prevention).
Write arch card JSON files with a plain writeFile — use write-to-temp-then-rename (atomic) wrapped in withArchLock (serialized) to prevent concurrent-write corruption.
Ignore ENOENT on card lookup and let the error propagate silently.
decisions: 2026-05-19: assertSafeSlug(/^[a-z0-9-]+$/) + safeArchCardPath (resolves and bounds-checks) added in both arch.ts and arch-server.mjs.
2026-05-19: writeArch/writeArchCard now write to a randomBytes temp file then fs.rename for atomicity; withArchLock serializes concurrent writes per card path.
